data.current_description:
To track an ocean shipment, you create a new tracking request. Two attributes are required to track a shipment. A `bill of lading/booking number` and a shipping line `SCAC`. Once a tracking request is created we will attempt to fetch the shipment details and it's related containers from the shipping line. If the attempt is successful we will create in new shipment object including any related container objects. We will send a `tracking_request.succeeded` webhook notification to your webhooks. If the attempt to fetch fails then we will send a `tracking_request.failed` webhook notification to your `webhooks`. A `tracking_request.succeeded` or `tracking_request.failed` webhook notificaiton will only be sent if you have atleast one active webhook.

This endpoint is limited to 100 tracking requests per minute.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new tracking request to begin monitoring an ocean shipment. Requires a bill of lading/booking number and shipping line SCAC code. The system asynchronously fetches shipment details and related containers from the shipping line. Returns a tracking_request object immediately. Webhook notifications (tracking_request.succeeded or tracking_request.failed) are sent upon completion if at least one active webhook is configured. Rate limited to 100 requests per minute. Note: Successful requests create associated shipment and container objects.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic ('Create a tracking request'). Description has typos ('in new' should be 'a new', 'notificaiton' should be 'notification', 'atleast' should be 'at least') and lacks clarity about the immediate response.
